description_long: This is a freestanding water cooler with a rectangular, brown base and a large, translucent blue water bottle mounted on top. The front panel features two distinct push-button spouts, one red and one blue, likely for dispensing hot and cold water, set into a white section for easy visibility. The sides of the cooler are plain, while the rear panel is equipped with multiple ventilation slats and a black access panel near the bottom, suggesting internal cooling or heating components. The water bottle is cylindrical and slightly ribbed, typical of large-capacity water dispensers. The overall design is utilitarian, with a focus on functionality and durability, suitable for public or semi-public urban environments. No visible branding or cup holder attachments are present.
description: A brown, rectangular water cooler with a large blue water bottle on top and two colored push-button spouts on the front. The rear features ventilation slats and a black access panel.
description_view_0: Front view showing the water bottle, two colored push-button spouts, and a white control panel area.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the plain brown side and a slight protrusion of the spout buttons.
description_view_2: Rear view with vertical ventilation slats and a black access panel at the bottom.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, with the colored spout buttons faintly visible.
attribute_control_panel_design: Two push-button spouts (red and blue) on a white panel, no visible indicators or displays.
